summary: Iteration 12 — Frontend-only validation of the 9 new feature scenarios on https://software-staging.preview.emergentagent.com (PHP/MySQL/Bootstrap). ALL spec scenarios PASS. Verified: (1) Shop sidebar filter-sidebar with 3 groups (Category/Version-Year/OS) auto-submits on checkbox change; combining filter-cat-office + filter-ver-2024 + filter-os-mac yields EXACTLY 2 products (shop-result-count text '2 products found'); filters-clear link appears when filters active and resets to 37 products. (2) Sort select price_asc preserves filter checkboxes (cat=True, ver=True, os=True remained checked after sort). (3) View toggle: default shop-grid → view-list-btn switches to shop-list with 37 product-row-* rows; Add to cart from list view increments the navbar cart badge to '1' (visible in screenshot); switching back to grid restores shop-grid. (4) New logo: brand-logo data-testid contains an SVG .brand-mark + 'UCODE SOFTTECH' wordmark on the main navbar, footer (after scroll), AND the checkout slim header (loaded with item in cart). (5) Content pages are colorful: help-center has 4 clickable quick-link cards (installation/activation/refund/contact) + 1 table + 2 alerts + 3 badges; activation-help has 1 table + 4 colored alerts + 3 badges (Activation Limits table verified); faqs has 1 license-type table + 1 info card + 7 badges + 'UCODE SOFTTECH' wording; returns-refunds has 2 tables + 2 alerts + 5 badges + 'Start a Refund Request' button linking to returns.php. (6) Buttons functional: homepage has ZERO old page.php?slug=contact links, all 'Contact Sales/Support' now link to contact.php; 6 tel: links and 2 mailto: links found across header/topbar/trustbar; mega menu nav-microsoft opens correctly with a Volume Pricing 'Request a Quote' link pointing to contact.php. (7) Dark mode v2: theme-toggle flips data-bs-theme to 'dark', body background becomes rgb(7,12,25) deep navy palette, page-content alert text remains readable (rgb(117,183,152) on dark bg). (8) Regression: product.php?slug=microsoft-office-home-2024-mac shows 'Professional Plus' variant correctly blurred (opacity 0.45, filter blur(1px), pointer-events:none, class 'variant-blur'); contact form full submit (name/email/select subject/message) succeeds → contact-success alert renders. (9) Mobile 390px: filters-toggle is visible on shop.php, clicking expands filter-sidebar (visible=True); document.scrollWidth == 390 on both shop.php and index.php → no horizontal overflow.
critical_code_review_comments: Shop filter logic in /app/php-version/shop.php correctly intersects the three GET arrays (cat[], ver[], os[]) — combined filter office+2024+Mac correctly returns 2 products (Office Home 2024 Mac, Office 2024 Professional Plus Mac). product_family() and product_version() helpers properly drive the version chip mapping.

render_logo() helper in includes/functions.php produces a single source of truth used by the main navbar, footer, and checkout slim header — verified present at all three locations including the slim header which historically tends to be forgotten.

Dark mode v2 palette is applied via data-bs-theme switching only on <html>, not relying on a 'dark' class — clean Bootstrap-native approach. Alert/table readability on .page-content survives the switch (no hardcoded light backgrounds bleeding through).

Mobile filter UX uses Bootstrap collapse (data-bs-toggle=collapse, data-bs-target=#shopFilters) so the sidebar is keyboard/AT-accessible and progressive-enhanced. Toggle is correctly hidden on lg+ via d-lg-none.

Auto-submit-on-change for checkboxes and sort select is implemented via inline onchange='this.form.submit()' — simple and works without JS framework, but consider preserving scroll position via History API to avoid jumping to top of page on each toggle (minor UX nit; not in scope).

Volume Pricing band in the Microsoft Products mega menu now links to contact.php instead of page.php?slug=quote — fully consistent with the rest of the homepage CTAs (0 legacy slug=contact links remain on /index.php).

Variant chip blur on Office 2024 Home Mac (Professional Plus) is implemented via class 'variant-blur' with pointer-events:none — correct UX to indicate the SKU exists but is not selectable in this family.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@ucodesofttechus.com / Admin@UC2026! (not exercised this round — frontend-only run). Guest contact form used qa-iter12@example.com.
seed_data_creation: 1 row inserted into support_messages (name='QA Bot', email='qa-iter12@example.com', source='contact') as a side-effect of the contact form regression test. Cleanly identifiable by email prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All shop filter+view+sort+logo+dark-v2+content-pages+mobile scenarios are working end-to-end. Key data-testids verified present and behaving: filter-sidebar, filter-cat-{office|windows|apps|antivirus}, filter-ver-{2024|2021|2019|windows-11|windows-10}, filter-os-{windows|mac}, shop-result-count, filters-clear, view-grid-btn, view-list-btn, shop-grid, shop-list, product-row-{slug}, sort-select, brand-logo (+ .brand-mark), theme-toggle, filters-toggle, contact-name/email/subject(select)/message/send, contact-success. The list view yields 37 product-row elements when no filters applied (= full catalog count). 'Quick Links' wording is no longer a literal string on help-center but the 4 quick-link cards (installation/activation/refund/contact) are rendered correctly — keyword search on raw text may falsely negative; use card selector instead. Cart count badge is rendered by the navbar partial; selector '.cart-count' / '#cart-count' was NOT a direct match in this run (the visible badge is a Bootstrap pill inside the Cart link) — if a future test needs to assert the count programmatically, target the link's inner text e.g. 'a[data-testid=cart-link] .badge' or read window.localStorage.cart length.
rca of the issue: n/a — no functional issues. The transient 'cart-count selector not found' INFO line during list-view add-to-cart was a test-script artifact: the click DID succeed (the Cart pill badge visibly flipped to '1' in the captured screenshot), only the assertion selector was over-specific. App behavior is correct.
